description: CLI Scanner Engine - Three-tier deterministic parser that scans arbitrary CLI tools into structured metadata

tasks.title: ValueType enum and HelpFormat enum
tasks.description: Define the ValueType enum (String, Integer, Float, Boolean, Path, Enum, Url, Unknown) and HelpFormat enum (Gnu, Click, Argparse, Cobra, Clap, Unknown) in src/models/mod.rs with serde rename_all lowercase.
tasks.test_first: Write tests that ValueType and HelpFormat variants serialize/deserialize correctly to/from JSON lowercase strings (e.g., ValueType::Boolean -> "boolean"). Test Debug and Clone derive. Test PartialEq comparisons.
tasks.implementation: Define both enums with #[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, Serialize, Deserialize)] and #[serde(rename_all = "lowercase")]. Add all variants per spec.
tasks.acceptance: All serde round-trip tests pass. Enums compile with required derives.

tasks.title: ScannedFlag struct and canonical_name()
tasks.description: Define ScannedFlag struct with all fields (long_name, short_name, description, value_type, required, default, enum_values, repeatable, value_name). Implement canonical_name() method.
tasks.test_first: Test canonical_name(): --message -> "message", --dry-run -> "dry_run", -m (short only) -> "m", neither -> "unknown". Test serde round-trip for ScannedFlag with all fields populated and with optional fields as None.
tasks.implementation: Define ScannedFlag struct with Serialize/Deserialize derives. Implement canonical_name() that prefers long_name, strips --, replaces - with _.
tasks.acceptance: canonical_name tests pass for all edge cases. Serde round-trip works.

tasks.title: ScannedArg struct
tasks.description: Define ScannedArg struct with fields: name, description, value_type, required, variadic.
tasks.test_first: Test serde round-trip for ScannedArg. Test a variadic arg (variadic: true) and a required arg (required: true) serialize correctly.
tasks.implementation: Define ScannedArg with derives and all fields per spec.
tasks.acceptance: Serde tests pass. Struct compiles with Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize.

tasks.title: StructuredOutputInfo struct
tasks.description: Define StructuredOutputInfo with fields: supported (bool), flag (Option<String>), format (Option<String>). Implement Default (supported: false, others None).
tasks.test_first: Test Default::default() gives supported=false, flag=None, format=None. Test serde round-trip with supported=true, flag="--json", format="json".
tasks.implementation: Define struct with Default derive and Serialize/Deserialize.
tasks.acceptance: Default and serde tests pass.

tasks.title: ScannedCommand struct
tasks.description: Define ScannedCommand with all fields: name, full_command, description, flags, positional_args, subcommands (recursive), examples, help_format, structured_output, raw_help.
tasks.test_first: Test serde round-trip for a ScannedCommand with nested subcommands (at least 2 levels deep). Test empty subcommands list. Test that all field types are correct.
tasks.implementation: Define ScannedCommand struct. The subcommands field is Vec<ScannedCommand> enabling recursive nesting.
tasks.acceptance: Nested serde round-trip works. Struct compiles with all derives.

tasks.title: ScannedCLITool struct
tasks.description: Define the top-level ScannedCLITool struct with: name, binary_path, version, subcommands, global_flags, structured_output, scan_tier, warnings.
tasks.test_first: Test serde round-trip for ScannedCLITool with populated subcommands and global_flags. Test with version=None. Test warnings as Vec<String>.
tasks.implementation: Define ScannedCLITool struct with all fields per tech design section 7.2.2.
tasks.acceptance: Full model hierarchy (ScannedCLITool -> ScannedCommand -> nested) serializes to JSON and back.

tasks.title: ParsedHelp struct
tasks.description: Define ParsedHelp struct (intermediate parser output) with: description, flags, positional_args, subcommand_names, examples, structured_output. Implement Default.
tasks.test_first: Test Default::default() gives empty strings/vecs and default StructuredOutputInfo. Test serde round-trip with populated fields.
tasks.implementation: Define ParsedHelp in src/scanner/protocol.rs with Default derive.
tasks.acceptance: Default and serde tests pass.

tasks.title: CliParser trait definition
tasks.description: Define the CliParser trait with methods: name() -> &str, priority() -> u32, can_parse(help_text, tool_name) -> bool, parse(help_text, tool_name) -> Result<ParsedHelp>. Trait must be Send + Sync.
tasks.test_first: Create a MockParser implementing CliParser. Test that name(), priority(), can_parse(), and parse() work correctly. Verify the mock can be stored as Box<dyn CliParser> (Send + Sync).
tasks.implementation: Define the trait in src/scanner/protocol.rs with the four required methods.
tasks.acceptance: MockParser compiles, implements all methods, and is object-safe (Box<dyn CliParser>).

tasks.title: ResolvedTool struct
tasks.description: Define ResolvedTool struct with: name, binary_path, version (Option<String>).
tasks.test_first: Test serde round-trip for ResolvedTool with version=Some and version=None.
tasks.implementation: Define struct in src/scanner/resolver.rs with Serialize/Deserialize.
tasks.acceptance: Serde tests pass.

tasks.title: ToolResolver - resolve binary path
tasks.description: Implement ToolResolver::resolve() that uses which::which to find binary path on PATH. Return Err(ToolNotFound) if not found.
tasks.test_first: Test resolve("sh") or resolve("echo") returns Ok with a valid binary_path. Test resolve("zzz_no_such_tool_xyz") returns Err(ToolNotFound).
tasks.implementation: Implement resolve() using which::which(). Map the which error to ApexeError::ToolNotFound. Build ResolvedTool with name and binary_path.
tasks.acceptance: Known tool resolves. Unknown tool returns ToolNotFound error.

tasks.title: ToolResolver - get_version()
tasks.description: Implement get_version() that runs <binary> --version, parses first line for semver-like pattern (\d+\.\d+[\.\d]*).
tasks.test_first: Test with mock output strings: "git version 2.43.0" -> Some("2.43.0"), "curl 8.1.2 (...)" -> Some("8.1.2"), "no version here" -> None, empty string -> None.
tasks.implementation: Run Command::new(binary).arg("--version").output(). Parse first line with regex. Return Option<String>.
tasks.acceptance: Version extraction works for common patterns. Returns None for unparseable output.

tasks.title: Help format detection function
tasks.description: Implement detect_help_format() that identifies GNU, Click, Cobra, Clap, or Unknown from help text heuristics.
tasks.test_first: Test with sample help texts: Cobra text containing 'Available Commands:' -> HelpFormat::Cobra. Clap text with 'SUBCOMMANDS:' -> HelpFormat::Clap. Click text with '[OPTIONS]' and 'Commands:' -> HelpFormat::Click. GNU text with 'Usage:' -> HelpFormat::Gnu. Random text -> HelpFormat::Unknown.
tasks.implementation: Implement the cascading if/else heuristic per tech design section 7.2.6.
tasks.acceptance: All five format types detected correctly from representative samples.

tasks.title: GNU parser - can_parse() detection
tasks.description: Implement GnuHelpParser struct and its can_parse() method that returns true for GNU-style help and false for Cobra/Clap/Click.
tasks.test_first: Test can_parse with real-ish GNU help (git commit --help style) -> true. Test with Cobra help (Available Commands:) -> false. Test with Clap help (SUBCOMMANDS:) -> false. Test with empty string -> false.
tasks.implementation: Implement GnuHelpParser as unit struct. can_parse checks for 'Usage:' presence, GNU option patterns, and excludes Cobra/Clap markers.
tasks.acceptance: Correctly identifies GNU help and rejects other formats.

tasks.title: GNU parser - extract_description()
tasks.description: Implement extract_description() that extracts the description paragraph before Usage:/Options: lines, capped at 200 chars.
tasks.test_first: Test: help text with description before 'Usage:' line extracts the description. Test: help starting with 'Usage:' returns empty. Test: long description is truncated to 200 chars.
tasks.implementation: Iterate lines, collect non-empty lines before first Usage:/Options: line, join with space, truncate to 200 chars.
tasks.acceptance: Description extraction works for standard GNU help layouts.

tasks.title: GNU parser - extract_flags() basic
tasks.description: Implement extract_flags() using regex to parse GNU-style option lines like '  -m, --message=MSG  Use the given message'.
tasks.test_first: Test parsing: '  -m, --message MSG  Use the given message' -> short=-m, long=--message, value_type=String. '  -a, --all  Stage all' -> Boolean flag. '  --config FILE  Config path' -> value_type=Path. '  -n, --count NUM  Number' -> value_type=Integer.
tasks.implementation: Use regex pattern to match option lines. Extract short/long names, value placeholder, description. Map placeholders to ValueType.
tasks.acceptance: Basic flag extraction works for short+long, long-only, and boolean flags with correct type inference.

tasks.title: GNU parser - enum, default, required, repeatable detection
tasks.description: Enhance extract_flags() to detect enum values ({json,text,csv}), defaults ([default: 80]), required flags, and repeatable flags.
tasks.test_first: Test: description containing '{json,text,csv}' -> enum_values=[json,text,csv], value_type=Enum. '[default: 80]' -> default=Some("80"). 'required' in description -> required=true. 'can be repeated' -> repeatable=true.
tasks.implementation: Add regex patterns for default_re, enum_re. Check description text for required/repeatable keywords. Override value_type to Enum when enum_values present.
tasks.acceptance: All four detection patterns work correctly in isolation and combined.

tasks.title: GNU parser - extract_positional_args()
tasks.description: Implement extract_positional_args() that parses positional arguments from Usage: line patterns like <file>...
tasks.test_first: Test: 'Usage: tool <file>' -> one required arg named 'file'. 'Usage: tool <src> <dst>' -> two args. 'Usage: tool <file>...' -> variadic=true. 'Usage: tool [OPTIONS]' -> no positional args.
tasks.implementation: Regex match <name>(...)? patterns in Usage: lines. Create ScannedArg for each match.
tasks.acceptance: Positional args extracted correctly including variadic detection.

tasks.title: GNU parser - extract_subcommands()
tasks.description: Implement extract_subcommands() that finds command names from Commands/Subcommands sections.
tasks.test_first: Test: help text with 'Commands:\n  commit  Record changes\n  push  Upload changes' -> ["commit", "push"]. Test: no commands section -> empty vec. Test: section ends at blank line.
tasks.implementation: Find section header matching /commands|subcommands/i. Parse indented lines for command names until section boundary.
tasks.acceptance: Subcommand names extracted from standard GNU help layouts.

tasks.title: GNU parser - extract_examples()
tasks.description: Implement extract_examples() that finds example invocations from Examples: sections.
tasks.test_first: Test: help with 'Examples:\n  $ git commit -m "msg"\n  $ git commit --amend' -> two examples. Test: no examples section -> empty vec. Test: lines starting with $ or # are captured.
tasks.implementation: Find Examples/Usage examples section header. Collect lines starting with $ or # until blank line.
tasks.acceptance: Examples extracted from help text. Empty vec when no examples section.

tasks.title: GNU parser - full parse() integration
tasks.description: Wire up GnuHelpParser::parse() to call all extract_* functions and return a complete ParsedHelp. Include detect_structured_output().
tasks.test_first: Test with a comprehensive GNU-style help text (pre-captured git commit --help style) that exercises description, flags, args, subcommands, and structured output detection. Verify all ParsedHelp fields populated.
tasks.implementation: Implement parse() calling extract_description, extract_flags, extract_positional_args, extract_subcommands, extract_examples, detect_structured_output.
tasks.acceptance: Full parse of representative GNU help text returns correctly populated ParsedHelp.

tasks.title: Click/argparse parser
tasks.description: Implement ClickHelpParser with can_parse() and parse(). Handles Click-specific patterns: TEXT/INTEGER/FLOAT/PATH value types, --flag/--no-flag booleans, [required], [default: X], [opt1|opt2] enums.
tasks.test_first: Test can_parse with Click-style help (contains [OPTIONS] and Options: but not Available Commands:) -> true. Test parse() with pre-captured Click help: extract flags with TEXT->String, INTEGER->Integer, PATH->Path types. Test --flag/--no-flag -> Boolean. Test [required] and [default: X] detection.
tasks.implementation: Implement ClickHelpParser struct, can_parse (priority 110), and parse with Click-specific regex patterns for value types and metadata.
tasks.acceptance: Click help text parsed correctly with appropriate type mappings. Commands section parsed for subcommands.

tasks.title: Cobra (Go) parser
tasks.description: Implement CobraHelpParser with can_parse() and parse(). Handles Cobra patterns: 'Available Commands:' section, 'Flags:' section (not Options:), 'Global Flags:' section, inline type after flag name.
tasks.test_first: Test can_parse with Cobra help (Available Commands: present) -> true. Test parse() with pre-captured kubectl/docker-style help: extract Available Commands list, parse Flags section with inline types (--flag string Description), detect Global Flags separately.
tasks.implementation: Implement CobraHelpParser (priority 120). Parse Available Commands for subcommands, Flags section with type-after-flag pattern, Global Flags section.
tasks.acceptance: Cobra help parsed with subcommands, flags with inline types, and global flags.

tasks.title: Clap (Rust) parser
tasks.description: Implement ClapHelpParser with can_parse() and parse(). Handles Clap patterns: SUBCOMMANDS: (uppercase), Options: with <VALUE> placeholders, -h/--help always present.
tasks.test_first: Test can_parse with Clap help (SUBCOMMANDS: present or angle-bracket values with Options:) -> true. Test parse() with pre-captured ripgrep-style help: extract SUBCOMMANDS list, parse options with <VALUE> syntax.
tasks.implementation: Implement ClapHelpParser (priority 130). Parse SUBCOMMANDS section, Options with <VALUE> placeholders.
tasks.acceptance: Clap help parsed with subcommands and flags using angle-bracket value names.

tasks.title: StructuredOutputDetector
tasks.description: Implement StructuredOutputDetector that checks parsed flags and raw help text for JSON output support patterns (--format, --json, -j, etc.).
tasks.test_first: Test: flag with long_name=--format and enum_values containing 'json' -> supported=true, flag='--format json'. Test: flag --json -> supported=true. Test: no json flags -> supported=false. Test: regex fallback on help text containing '--json' pattern.
tasks.implementation: Implement detect() method checking flags first (enum_values for json), then regex patterns on raw help text.
tasks.acceptance: Structured output correctly detected from both parsed flags and raw help text patterns.

tasks.title: ParserPipeline - initialization with built-in parsers
tasks.description: Implement ParserPipeline::new() that registers all built-in parsers (GNU, Click, Cobra, Clap) sorted by priority, with optional plugin parsers.
tasks.test_first: Test new(None) creates pipeline with 4 built-in parsers. Test new(Some(vec![mock_plugin])) includes the plugin. Test parsers are sorted by priority ascending.
tasks.implementation: Create ParserPipeline struct holding Vec<Box<dyn CliParser>>. In new(), push all built-in parsers, extend with plugins, sort by priority.
tasks.acceptance: Pipeline initialized with correct parser count and priority ordering.

tasks.title: ParserPipeline - parse() priority routing
tasks.description: Implement ParserPipeline::parse() that tries parsers in priority order, using first matching parser. Falls back to raw help text if none match.
tasks.test_first: Test: GNU help text -> uses GnuHelpParser. Cobra help text -> uses CobraHelpParser. Test: all parsers reject -> returns fallback ParsedHelp with truncated raw text as description. Test: parser that can_parse but fails parse() -> tries next parser.
tasks.implementation: Iterate parsers, call can_parse(), then parse(). On parse error, log warning and continue. If none match, return fallback ParsedHelp.
tasks.acceptance: Priority routing selects correct parser. Fallback works when no parser matches.

tasks.title: ParserPipeline - user override support
tasks.description: Implement user_override parameter in parse() that loads a YAML file as ParsedHelp, bypassing all parsers.
tasks.test_first: Test: provide a temp YAML file with ParsedHelp content -> returns parsed YAML content, skips all parsers. Test: override path does not exist -> falls through to parser pipeline. Test: override is invalid YAML -> falls through to parsers.
tasks.implementation: In parse(), check user_override path first. If exists and valid YAML, deserialize and return. Otherwise proceed to parsers.
tasks.acceptance: User override YAML loaded when present. Gracefully falls through on missing/invalid override.

tasks.title: SubcommandDiscovery - basic recursive discovery
tasks.description: Implement SubcommandDiscovery struct that recursively discovers subcommands by running <tool> <subcmd> --help and parsing results.
tasks.test_first: Test with mocked run_help (inject help text instead of running subprocess): given top-level subcommand_names ["sub1", "sub2"], mock sub1 help returning flags and no nested subcommands, mock sub2 help returning nested subcommand "sub2a". Verify correct ScannedCommand tree built with nesting.
tasks.implementation: Implement discover() that iterates subcommand_names, calls run_help for each, parses with pipeline, recursively discovers nested subcommands. Build ScannedCommand for each.
tasks.acceptance: Recursive discovery builds correct command tree. Nested subcommands resolved.

tasks.title: SubcommandDiscovery - max depth enforcement
tasks.description: Implement max_depth guard in discover() to prevent infinite recursion.
tasks.test_first: Test with max_depth=1: top-level subcommands discovered, but their nested subcommands are NOT discovered (returns empty vec at depth >= max_depth). Test with max_depth=0 returns empty immediately.
tasks.implementation: Check depth >= max_depth at start of discover(). If exceeded, log warning and return empty Vec.
tasks.acceptance: Recursion stops at max_depth. No infinite loops possible.

tasks.title: SubcommandDiscovery - stderr fallback for help
tasks.description: Implement run_help() to capture both stdout and stderr, using stderr when stdout is empty (some tools output help to stderr).
tasks.test_first: Test: tool with help on stdout -> returns stdout. Test: tool with empty stdout but help on stderr -> returns stderr. Test: both empty -> returns None.
tasks.implementation: In run_help(), run Command, check stdout first, fall back to stderr. Return None if both empty.
tasks.acceptance: Help text captured regardless of stdout/stderr destination.

tasks.title: Man page parser (Tier 2)
tasks.description: Implement ManPageParser::parse_man_page() that runs 'man -P cat <tool>' and extracts DESCRIPTION section.
tasks.test_first: Test extract_man_description with sample man page text containing DESCRIPTION section -> extracts first paragraph. Test with no DESCRIPTION section -> returns empty. Test parse_man_page returns None when man command fails.
tasks.implementation: Run man -P cat <tool>. Parse output for DESCRIPTION section. Return ParsedHelp with description populated.
tasks.acceptance: Man page description extracted. Returns None when man page unavailable.

tasks.title: Shell completion parser (Tier 3)
tasks.description: Implement CompletionParser::parse_completions() that reads zsh/bash completion files and extracts subcommand names.
tasks.test_first: Test extract_completion_subcommands with sample zsh completion content containing subcommand definitions -> returns subcommand names. Test with nonexistent completion files -> returns None.
tasks.implementation: Check known completion file paths. Read and parse completion script for subcommand patterns. Return ParsedHelp with subcommand_names.
tasks.acceptance: Completion file read and basic subcommand extraction works. Graceful None on missing files.

tasks.title: ScanCache - store and retrieve
tasks.description: Implement ScanCache with get() and put() methods for filesystem-based caching of ScannedCLITool as JSON.
tasks.test_first: Test: put() a ScannedCLITool, then get() with same tool_name and version -> returns the stored tool. Test: get() with wrong version -> None. Test: get() on empty cache -> None. Use tempdir for cache_dir.
tasks.implementation: Cache key is {tool_name}_{version}.scan.json. put() creates dir, writes JSON. get() reads and deserializes.
tasks.acceptance: Round-trip cache store/load works. Cache miss returns None.

tasks.title: ScanCache - invalidation
tasks.description: Implement ScanCache::invalidate() that removes all cached results for a given tool name.
tasks.test_first: Test: put() two versions of same tool, invalidate(tool_name), then get() both -> None. Test: invalidate on empty cache -> no error.
tasks.implementation: Read cache_dir, remove files matching {tool_name}_*.scan.json pattern.
tasks.acceptance: All versions of tool removed from cache. No errors on empty cache.

tasks.title: Plugin system - discover_parser_plugins()
tasks.description: Implement discover_parser_plugins() that scans plugins directory for .so/.dylib files. Returns empty vec when no plugins or directory missing.
tasks.test_first: Test: empty plugins dir -> returns empty vec. Test: nonexistent dir -> returns empty vec. Test: dir with non-.so files -> returns empty vec. Test: function returns Vec<Box<dyn CliParser>>.
tasks.implementation: Read plugins dir, filter for .so/.dylib extensions, attempt load_plugin for each. Log warnings on failures. Sort by priority.
tasks.acceptance: Plugin discovery handles all edge cases gracefully. Returns empty vec when no valid plugins.

tasks.title: Plugin system - programmatic registration
tasks.description: Implement the ability to register custom CliParser implementations programmatically via ParserPipeline::new(Some(plugins)).
tasks.test_first: Test: create two mock parsers with different priorities. Register via new(Some(vec![...])). Call parse() with text that only mock1 can handle -> mock1.parse() called. Test priority ordering: lower priority parser used first.
tasks.implementation: ParserPipeline::new already accepts Option<Vec<Box<dyn CliParser>>>. Ensure plugins are merged with built-ins and sorted.
tasks.acceptance: Custom parsers registered and used in correct priority order alongside built-ins.

tasks.title: ScanOrchestrator - construction
tasks.description: Implement ScanOrchestrator::new() that wires together ToolResolver, ParserPipeline, ScanCache, ManPageParser, and CompletionParser from ApexeConfig.
tasks.test_first: Test: construct ScanOrchestrator with a test config (temp dirs). Verify it can be created without panicking. Test that cache_dir from config is used.
tasks.implementation: Implement new() that initializes all sub-components from config fields.
tasks.acceptance: Orchestrator constructed successfully from config. All components initialized.

tasks.title: ScanOrchestrator - single tool scan (Tier 1)
tasks.description: Implement scan() for a single tool: resolve binary, run --help, parse with pipeline, discover subcommands, build ScannedCLITool.
tasks.test_first: Test with a commonly available tool (e.g., 'echo' or 'ls'): scan returns ScannedCLITool with name, binary_path, and scan_tier=1. Test that help text is parsed (at least description or flags present).
tasks.implementation: In scan(), iterate tool_names: resolve -> run --help -> parse -> discover subcommands -> build ScannedCLITool with tier=1.
tasks.acceptance: Single tool scanned end-to-end. ScannedCLITool populated with basic metadata.

tasks.title: ScanOrchestrator - cache integration
tasks.description: Implement cache check in scan(): skip scanning if cached result exists, unless no_cache=true.
tasks.test_first: Test: scan a tool, scan again -> second scan returns cached result (verify by checking that no subprocess would be needed). Test: scan with no_cache=true -> always runs fresh scan even if cached.
tasks.implementation: Before scanning, check cache.get(). If hit and !no_cache, return cached. After scan, call cache.put().
tasks.acceptance: Cache hit skips scanning. no_cache flag forces re-scan.

tasks.title: ScanOrchestrator - Tier 2/3 enrichment
tasks.description: Implement man page (Tier 2) and shell completion (Tier 3) enrichment in scan(). Enrich sparse Tier 1 results with additional metadata.
tasks.test_first: Test: mock man_parser returning Some(ParsedHelp) with a description -> tool.scan_tier set to 2 when Tier 1 description was sparse. Test: mock completion_parser returning Some -> scan_tier bumped to 3.
tasks.implementation: After Tier 1 scan, call man_parser.parse_man_page(). If result has useful data and Tier 1 was sparse, merge. Then call completion_parser for Tier 3.
tasks.acceptance: Tier 2/3 enrichment upgrades scan_tier. Man page descriptions merged when Tier 1 sparse.

tasks.title: ScanOrchestrator - multi-tool scan
tasks.description: Implement scanning multiple tools in a single scan() call, returning Vec<ScannedCLITool>.
tasks.test_first: Test: scan(["echo", "ls"]) returns two ScannedCLITool results. Test: one tool not found -> returns error (or partial results depending on error strategy).
tasks.implementation: Iterate tool_names, scan each, collect results. Propagate ToolNotFound error.
tasks.acceptance: Multiple tools scanned in one call. Results returned for each.

tasks.title: Error handling - scan timeout and permissions
tasks.description: Implement timeout handling for --help subprocess calls and graceful error handling for permission-denied scenarios.
tasks.test_first: Test: mock a slow --help that would exceed timeout -> scan returns warning or error. Test: tool that returns non-zero exit code for --help -> still captures help from stderr. Test: empty help output -> warning added to ScannedCLITool.warnings.
tasks.implementation: Add timeout to Command execution (via std::time::Duration). Handle non-zero exit codes by checking stderr. Add warnings to ScannedCLITool.warnings vec.
tasks.acceptance: Timeouts handled gracefully. Non-zero exit codes for --help handled. Warnings populated.

tasks.title: GNU parser - nom-based flag line parser
tasks.description: Implement parse_flag_line() using nom combinators as an alternative/supplement to regex-based extraction for more robust parsing.
tasks.test_first: Test parse_flag_line: '  -m, --message=MSG   Use the given message' -> (Some("m"), Some("message"), Some("MSG"), "Use the given message"). '  --all  Stage all' -> (None, Some("all"), None, "Stage all"). '  -v  Verbose' -> (Some("v"), None, None, "Verbose").
tasks.implementation: Use nom combinators: multispace0, char('-'), take_while1, opt, preceded, tag("--") to parse flag line structure.
tasks.acceptance: Nom parser handles all GNU flag line variants including short-only, long-only, combined, with/without values.

tasks.title: Integration test - scan git
tasks.description: End-to-end integration test that scans 'git' and verifies the command tree structure.
tasks.test_first: Test (marked #[ignore] for CI): scan("git") produces ScannedCLITool with name="git", version present, subcommands including 'commit', 'push', 'pull', 'clone'. Verify 'git commit' has --message flag. Verify subcommand count > 20.
tasks.implementation: Integration test using real git binary. Assert key structural properties of the scan result.
tasks.acceptance: Git scan produces complete command tree with 50+ subcommands, flags on key commands verified.

tasks.title: Integration test - scan docker (nested subcommands)
tasks.description: End-to-end integration test for docker with nested subcommand discovery (docker container ls).
tasks.test_first: Test (marked #[ignore]): scan("docker") with depth=2 discovers nested subcommands. Verify 'docker container' has subcommand 'ls'. Verify 'docker container ls' has --format flag.
tasks.implementation: Integration test using real docker binary. Assert nested command tree structure.
tasks.acceptance: Docker scan discovers multi-level subcommand tree.

tasks.title: Integration test - graceful degradation
tasks.description: Test scanning a tool with unusual or minimal help output to verify graceful fallback.
tasks.test_first: Test: scan a tool whose help output doesn't match any parser -> ScannedCLITool returned with raw description, empty flags, warnings populated. No panic or crash.
tasks.implementation: Use a tool with non-standard help or mock a binary that outputs unusual text. Verify fallback behavior.
tasks.acceptance: Scanner degrades gracefully. No crashes on unparseable help. Warnings present.
